content.problemStatement:
Hola a todos, espero que todo vaya bien!!

Ved claims to be a pro at programming, but his friend Varun disagrees. To settle the debate, they decided to seek advice from their mentor. The mentor proposed a simple challenge:

Ved must write a program containing NNN lines of code. When the code is compiled, the compiler will indicate how many of those lines have errors, denoted as MMM. Based on the results:


If errors are present in at least **half** of the total lines, Ved will be labeled as a NEWBIE\text{NEWBIE}NEWBIE.
Otherwise, he will be called a PRO\text{PRO}PRO
After compiling Ved's code, the compiler reported errors in MMM lines. Determine Ved's skill category based on this evaluation.


Input Format

The input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of lines of code written by Ved and the number of lines of code containing errors, respectively.


Output Format
Output PRO\text{PRO}PRO if Ved is pro, else output NEWBIE\text{NEWBIE}NEWBIE.


It is allowed to print each character in either case. pro,pRo,PRo\text{pro}, \text{pRo}, \text{PRo}pro,pRo,PRo will all be accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There were 101010 lines and Ved has errors in 666 of them. Since 6≥56 \ge 56≥5, where 555 is half of 101010, Ved is a newbie due to having errors in at least half the lines.

Explanation:
Half of 999 is 4.54.54.5. Ved has errors in less than half the lines, thus he is a pro.
